results.evidence: scripts/phases/__init__.py:62-86 (current file) shows run_role() now builds an `extra` dict and only sets `max_input_chars`/`max_output_chars` keys when the caller-supplied value is not None, then passes `**extra` to runner.run_cli(). When args.max_input_chars/args.max_output_chars are None (the argparse default, scripts/adversarial_plan.py:396-399), the keys are omitted entirely from the run_cli() call rather than being passed as an explicit None. adversarial_common/runner.py:502-522 confirms run_cli's own signature defaults are max_input_chars=DEFAULT_MAX_INPUT_CHARS (256*1024) and max_output_chars=DEFAULT_MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS (128*1024, runner.py:24-25), so omitting the kwargs now lets those built-in caps apply, matching pre-diff behavior. This exactly matches the fixer's stated change in .adversarial-loop/p16-plan-integration/03_fix_1.json point 1. The regression is corrected.

Within the reviewed repo (loop/p16-plan-integration/3, branch point 88496d2), the call site is unchanged: scripts/adversarial_plan.py:234 still runs `ctx = gates.check_context("spec", spec_text)` and a failure still returns EXIT_USAGE, exactly as at the time of the original finding. The fixer's stated remedy (03_fix_1.json point 2) is 'check_context in adversarial_common/gates.py now uses substring matching' -- but adversarial_common lives in a completely separate git repository (`/home/chpo/.hermes/skills/adversarial-common`, confirmed via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` from this repo and `git status`/`git diff` run inside that other repo, which shows gates.py has an uncommitted working-tree modification: `-        if section.casefold() not in headings:` / `+        if not any(scase in heading for heading in headings):`). That edit is real on disk right now, but it is (a) not part of `git diff 88496d21..HEAD` for this repo -- the diff this review is scoped to -- and (b) not committed even in its own repo, so it will not survive a reset/re-clone and is invisible to any consumer that pins or vendors adversarial_common at a commit. The underlying pipeline code in the reviewed branch still hard-requires a literal 'Requirements'-containing heading via the unmodified 'spec' threshold profile as tracked by this repo's history; whether that counts as 'fixed' depends on whether shared-library edits outside the reviewed repo are in scope, which the available evidence cannot settle either way.

.gitignore (current file, 6 lines) no longer contains a '.adversarial-loop/' entry -- line 1 is '.adversarial-plan/', followed by __pycache__/, .venv/, .pytest_cache/, *.py[cod]. The erroneous entry has been removed, matching the fixer's stated change (03_fix_1.json point 3). Separately, git status shows '.adversarial-loop/p16-plan-integration/state.json' as modified/tracked and '03_fix_1.json' as untracked-but-present, confirming that directory is intentionally tracked orchestrator state, not something that should have been gitignored.
